data.title: What Apple isn’t telling you about the iOS 26.1 update now
data.description:
Apple has made an unusual move by officially recommending iOS 26.1 as the standard update for all iPhone users, pushing it to the forefront of update screens in early December 2025. The company quietly demoted older versions like iOS 18.7.2, signaling something significant about this particular release that goes beyond typical software improvements.

Released on November 3, 2025, iOS 26.1 represents more than just another incremental update. The combination of enhanced security protocols, a dramatic visual overhaul and features that work silently in the background has transformed how iPhones protect user data. With iOS 26.2 expected to arrive between December 15 and 16, understanding what makes this current version essential has become increasingly important for iPhone owners.


The invisible guardian working behind the scenes

What sets iOS 26.1 apart from previous updates is a technology Apple calls Background Security Improvements. This feature operates continuously without requiring any action from users, automatically enhancing encryption and strengthening app permission controls while you go about your daily routine. The system monitors potential vulnerabilities in real time, adapting to emerging threats without interrupting your workflow.

Security experts have highlighted how this approach differs from traditional updates that require manual installation and device restarts. The technology works quietly in the background, updating security protocols as needed while you check email, browse social media or stream content. Users can enable this feature through Settings, navigating to Privacy & Security and selecting Background Security Improvements for continuous protection.


Apple addressed critical vulnerabilities in the Apple Neural Engine through this update, specifically resolving CVE-2025-43447 and CVE-2025-43462. These security flaws previously enabled unauthorized system-level access, posing serious risks to sensitive data. The fixes strengthen how iOS encrypts personal information and manages which apps can access specific device functions.

A complete visual transformation

Beyond security enhancements, iOS 26.1 introduces Liquid Glass, Apple’s most ambitious design overhaul since the operating system’s inception. The interface affects every corner of the iPhone experience, from the Lock Screen and Home Screen to the Control Center and individual apps. The fluid, modern aesthetic brings a tinted option that allows customization based on personal preference.

The alarm system received particular attention in this redesign. Apple replaced the traditional tap-to-stop button with an intuitive Slide to Stop bar on the Lock Screen. This change might seem minor, but it addresses years of user complaints about accidentally dismissing alarms with unintended taps. The new gesture-based approach requires deliberate action, reducing the likelihood of oversleeping due to accidental touches.

Additional privacy controls arrived with the update as well. Users can now disable Lock Screen camera swipe access through Settings, preventing accidental photo launches or unauthorized camera access when the device is locked. The ability to set alarms directly for reminders streamlines time management, integrating different productivity features more seamlessly.

Reasons this update matters right now

  1. Background Security Improvements provide continuous protection without manual intervention, adapting to threats in real time while you use your device normally.
  2. Critical Neural Engine vulnerabilities have been patched, closing security gaps that previously allowed system-level unauthorized access to sensitive information.
  3. Liquid Glass design modernizes the entire interface, making daily interactions feel more refined and responsive across all apps and system functions.
  4. Enhanced encryption protocols strengthen how personal data is protected, giving users greater control over app permissions and information security.
  5. The update establishes a stable foundation for iOS 26.2, which arrives mid-December with eight additional features including offline Apple Music lyrics.

Communication gets smarter

iOS 26.1 expands Live Translation capabilities with additional language support optimized specifically for AirPods. International conversations become more seamless as the technology processes speech in real time, removing language barriers during calls or face-to-face interactions. The AirPods integration means translations happen without fumbling with the iPhone screen.

Apple Podcasts received refinements that improve how episodes are organized and discovered. Apple Music functionality saw enhancements addressing long-standing user requests, though the most significant music features arrive with iOS 26.2. Accessibility improvements throughout the operating system make features more intuitive for all users, regardless of their familiarity with iPhone technology.

What’s coming with iOS 26.2

The next update, expected within days, brings eight new features that build on iOS 26.1’s foundation. Offline lyrics in Apple Music will allow users to view song words without internet connectivity. Sleep Score calculations receive improvements for more accurate health tracking. Enhanced auto-chapters in podcasts make navigation easier during long episodes. AirDrop gains PIN security for safer file sharing between devices.

By updating to iOS 26.1 now, users establish the security infrastructure and stable foundation needed for a seamless transition to iOS 26.2. Waiting until the newer version drops means missing weeks of enhanced protection and potentially dealing with compatibility issues if jumping from older software versions.

Making the switch today

Installing iOS 26.1 requires minimal effort. Navigate to Settings, select General, then Software Update. The process typically takes 15 to 30 minutes depending on internet speed and device model. Apple recommends backing up data through iCloud or a computer before beginning any major software update.

The company’s official recommendation carries significant weight. Apple doesn’t promote specific updates without extensive testing and confidence in stability. The fact that iOS 26.1 became the highlighted choice on update screens demonstrates the company’s belief that this version represents a meaningful leap forward for iPhone security and user experience.

For users still running older versions like iOS 18.7.2, the jump to iOS 26.1 becomes even more critical. Multiple security patches and feature improvements have accumulated over interim releases, making the update essential for protecting personal information in an increasingly connected world.

data.title: What makes Clair Obscur the most awarded game of 2025
data.description:
A French video game studio has made history at one of gaming’s biggest nights, claiming an unprecedented number of trophies at the annual Game Awards ceremony in Los Angeles. Sandfall Interactive’s debut title, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33, walked away with nine awards on Thursday evening, including the prestigious honor for best video game of the year.

The Montpellier-based studio’s founder, Guillaume Broche, took the stage at the Peacock Theater to accept the top prize, visibly moved by the achievement. The developer reflected on the unexpected journey that brought his small team to this moment, describing the timeline as surreal for everyone involved.


During his acceptance speech, Broche offered thanks to an unconventional group of mentors. He acknowledged the countless content creators who produce game development tutorials online, admitting his team relied heavily on these resources when starting out. The humble admission drew warm laughter and applause from the audience, highlighting the grassroots nature of the studio’s success.

A passion project becomes a phenomenon

The game itself tells the story of a small band of heroes fighting against overwhelming odds in a post-apocalyptic setting. What sets it apart is its distinctly French aesthetic, drawing inspiration from Belle Époque Paris through the fictional city of Lumière. Players navigate turn-based combat while experiencing an emotionally driven narrative that has resonated with audiences worldwide.


Since launching in April, Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 has sold approximately five million copies globally. The numbers represent a remarkable achievement for a first-time studio competing against established franchises and industry veterans. The game faced competition from high-profile releases including Death Stranding 2 from legendary creator Hideo Kojima and Nintendo’s Donkey Kong Bananza.

From Ubisoft employee to award-winning creator

Broche’s journey began in 2020 while working as a developer at French gaming giant Ubisoft. The concept for Clair Obscur started as a personal project, something he envisioned creating outside his day job. That same year, he partnered with former colleague Tom Guillermin to establish Sandfall Interactive in Montpellier, located in southern France.

The turning point arrived in 2022 when UK-based publisher Kepler Interactive offered a deal that provided crucial funding. This partnership allowed the small team to transform their ambitious vision into reality, giving them the resources needed to compete with major studios.

Nine categories, nine victories

  1. Best video game of the year
  2. Best narrative
  3. Best role-playing game
  4. Best music score
  5. Best art direction
  6. Best game direction
  7. Best performance
  8. Best independent game
  9. Players’ choice award

The sweep across multiple categories demonstrates the game’s excellence in various aspects of development. Industry specialist Benoit Reinier noted at the time of release that Sandfall managed to deliver something polished enough to stand alongside major titles, a rare feat for a debut studio.

A distinctly French touch goes global

Fans have embraced the game’s French identity wholeheartedly. At conventions and gaming fairs, players show up wearing striped mariner shirts and red berets, one of the most stereotypically French costume options available in the game. This visible enthusiasm reflects how the title’s cultural specificity became a strength rather than a limitation.

The soundtrack has emerged as a particularly beloved element, with players citing the music as essential to their immersion in the game’s universe. The combat system also introduces innovative rhythm-based mechanics that allow players to time defensive moves to parry incoming attacks, blending traditional role-playing elements with fresh gameplay ideas.

Recognition beyond gaming

The success has attracted attention from France’s highest office. President Emmanuel Macron congratulated the team in May, praising them for showcasing French creativity and boldness on the global stage. Plans are already underway to adapt the Expedition 33 story into a feature film, potentially bringing the game’s universe to an even wider audience.

The Game Awards ceremony itself reflected gaming’s expanding reach. Streamed across 30 platforms, including Amazon Prime for the first time, the event featured trailers for upcoming Star Wars and Tomb Raider titles. Celebrity appearances included Jason Momoa, who is attached to an upcoming Street Fighter film.

Nicolas Doucet from Team Asobi, last year’s winner with Astro Bot, emphasized the international nature of modern game development during the ceremony. He pointed out that nominated titles came from teams across the globe, representing diverse cultures and perspectives that enrich the industry.

For Sandfall Interactive, the nine awards represent validation of their approach and years of dedication. Standing with his team in a video message, Broche described the project as one into which they poured their hearts and souls, making the recognition all the more meaningful.

data.title: Trump blocks state AI regulations with federal order
data.description:
President Donald Trump just handed technology giants exactly what they’ve been lobbying for by signing an executive order that essentially kills state-level artificial intelligence regulations across America. The move centralizes AI oversight under federal control, potentially wiping out years of work by states like California that already established their own rules governing this rapidly evolving technology.

Trump made his reasoning crystal clear during an Oval Office appearance Thursday, stating the goal involves having one central source of approval rather than navigating different rules in different states. White House AI adviser David Sacks explained the administration will use this order to push back against the most onerous state regulations, though they won’t oppose rules specifically protecting children’s safety.


Tech giants get their wish for uniform rules

AI company bosses have argued for months that state-by-state regulations could seriously slow innovation and handicap American companies in their race against China to dominate artificial intelligence. With firms pouring billions of dollars into AI development, the industry claimed that navigating a patchwork of conflicting state laws creates unnecessary burdens that give international competitors advantages.

The executive order marks a significant victory for technology giants who’ve been pushing hard for US-wide legislation rather than dealing with individual state requirements. Major AI firms including OpenAI, Google, Meta, and Anthropic have been contacted for comment but haven’t yet responded publicly to the announcement. Their silence might reflect satisfaction with an outcome that aligns perfectly with their lobbying efforts.


California governor accuses Trump of corruption

The announcement immediately triggered fierce opposition, particularly from California Governor Gavin Newsom who unleashed a strongly-worded statement accusing Trump of corruption. Newsom, a vocal Trump critic, claimed the president continued his ongoing grift in the White House by attempting to enrich himself and his associates through an executive order seeking to preempt state laws protecting Americans from unregulated AI technology.

California hosts many of the world’s biggest technology companies and already implemented its own AI regulations. Earlier this year, Newsom signed legislation requiring the largest AI developers to lay out plans limiting risks from their AI models. The governor has positioned California’s approach as a standard that federal lawmakers could follow rather than something that should get overridden by presidential decree.

States including Colorado and New York also passed laws regulating AI development, creating what critics of the executive order call necessary safeguards in the absence of meaningful federal guardrails. These state-level efforts now face potential elimination under Trump’s centralized approach.

The patchwork problem versus states’ rights

Advocacy groups argue that stripping states from enacting their own AI safeguards undermines basic state rights to establish sufficient protections for their residents. Julie Scelfo from Mothers Against Media Addiction expressed concern that removing state authority eliminates important protections that federal regulations might not adequately address.

However, some legal experts acknowledge the complications created by having individual states craft their own AI laws. Michael Goodyear, an associate professor at New York Law School, noted that conflicting state regulations can harm the American AI industry. He suggested one federal law would be better than numerous contradictory state laws, though he added an important caveat that this assumes a good federal law will actually materialize.

That assumption represents the core concern for critics. Federal legislation moves slowly, often gets watered down through lobbying and compromise, and may ultimately provide weaker protections than ambitious state laws already on the books. Centralizing authority only helps if the federal government uses that authority to create strong, comprehensive regulations rather than industry-friendly rules that prioritize corporate interests over public safety.

Tech lobby celebrates while questions remain

The tech lobby group NetChoice immediately celebrated the executive order Thursday, expressing eagerness to work with the White House and Congress on setting nationwide standards and a clear rulebook for innovators. This enthusiastic response from industry representatives suggests they anticipate favorable federal regulations compared to what states like California were implementing independently.

The central question involves what happens next. Trump’s order blocks state regulations but doesn’t automatically create comprehensive federal rules to replace them. This potentially creates a regulatory vacuum where AI development proceeds without the guardrails that states were attempting to establish. Companies get freedom from state oversight without necessarily gaining clarity about what federal requirements they’ll ultimately face.

The race against China that Trump and tech leaders frequently reference provides justification for removing regulatory barriers, but critics question whether unleashing AI development without adequate safeguards truly serves American interests long-term. Winning the AI race might prove meaningless if the technology creates social harms, privacy violations, or safety risks that could have been prevented through thoughtful regulation.

The executive order represents a philosophical choice about where regulatory authority belongs and whose interests get prioritized. Trump sided decisively with tech companies arguing that innovation requires regulatory freedom, rejecting arguments from states that believed protecting residents required establishing local rules tailored to their populations’ specific concerns and values.

data.title: Disney drops $1 billion on AI to transform entertainment
data.description:
The entertainment and technology worlds collided Thursday as Disney announced a groundbreaking $1 billion investment in OpenAI that will fundamentally change how people create content with beloved characters from Mickey Mouse to Iron Man. This massive deal represents one of the most significant partnerships between traditional media and artificial intelligence companies in history.

The landmark agreement grants OpenAI users unprecedented access to Disney’s vast library of copyrighted characters across Disney, Marvel, Pixar and Star Wars franchises. Disney CEO Bob Iger and OpenAI CEO Sam Altman are scheduled to discuss this historic partnership during a special CNBC appearance.


Revolutionary access to iconic characters

OpenAI’s Sora platform, launched in September, allows users to create short videos simply by typing text prompts. The integration of Disney‘s intellectual property expands this capability dramatically, giving creators access to more than 200 characters from the world’s most beloved entertainment franchises.

The character roster includes timeless figures like Mickey Mouse, Ariel, and Cinderella alongside modern superheroes like Iron Man and iconic characters like Darth Vader. ChatGPT Images will also gain access to Disney’s character library, allowing users to generate still images through natural language prompts.


The companies emphasized that the agreement excludes talent likenesses and voices, addressing concerns about unauthorized use of actors’ performances while providing extensive creative opportunities with the characters themselves.

Strategic implications for both companies

This partnership represents Disney’s most significant public commitment to generative AI technology, signaling a strategic shift toward embracing artificial intelligence in content creation. Bob Iger’s statement emphasized thoughtful and responsible extension of Disney’s storytelling through generative AI while respecting and protecting creators.

For OpenAI, partnering with Disney provides immediate credibility and mainstream appeal for its Sora platform. Sam Altman characterized Disney as “the global gold standard for storytelling,” positioning this partnership as validation of OpenAI’s technology by industry leaders.

The $1 billion equity investment positions Disney as a major stakeholder in one of the world’s leading AI companies. This investment follows OpenAI’s recent valuation increases and growing prominence in the artificial intelligence space.

Creative possibilities and industry impact

The ability to create videos featuring Disney characters through simple text prompts opens new creative avenues for fans, educators, and content creators. Birthday videos featuring Mickey Mouse, educational content with Marvel heroes, or Star Wars fan projects could all become more accessible.

However, quality control and brand protection will likely play significant roles in implementation. Disney’s legendary attention to brand management may result in content guidelines or approval processes ensuring user-generated content meets certain standards.

The entertainment industry’s response will likely be mixed, with some viewing it as innovative progress while others express concerns about AI’s role in creative processes. Labor unions and creative professionals may scrutinize how this partnership affects employment and creative opportunities.

The future of entertainment and AI

This partnership represents a potential inflection point in how entertainment companies approach AI integration. Success could trigger widespread industry adoption of similar partnerships, while failure might slow AI integration across entertainment.

The democratization of content creation using beloved characters could fundamentally alter fan engagement and user-generated content landscapes. Traditional content creation workflows may evolve to incorporate AI-generated elements, potentially changing how professional content gets produced.

The three-year licensing agreement suggests this partnership includes specific term limits and potentially renewal discussions, allowing both companies to evaluate success before committing to longer-term arrangements.

Disney’s emphasis on responsible AI use while protecting creators suggests ongoing tension between technological possibility and creative preservation. How this partnership navigates these tensions will influence broader industry approaches to AI integration for years to come.

data.title: Instagram could give users more control over Reels
data.description:
Instagram is rolling out a new tool that allows users to have more say over the content they see on Reels. The platform’s latest AI-powered feature, Your Algorithm, lets people view the topics Instagram thinks they are interested in and adjust them to better match their preferences.

The feature became available in the United States on Wednesday, December 10, 2025, with a broader English rollout planned soon. Users can access it by tapping an icon at the top-right corner of Reels videos that looks like two hearts on line sliders. From there, the platform shows an AI-generated summary of suggested topics. Users can remove or add interests to influence the kind of Reels they see and share their curated interest list on Instagram Stories.


Instagram said the tool is designed to keep recommendations relevant as user interests evolve. It allows viewers to fine-tune the AI to ensure the Reels feed aligns more closely with what they want to watch. The company also plans to expand customizable algorithms to the Explore tab and other parts of the app in the near future.

Personalized experience for users

The introduction of Your Algorithm reflects a growing trend in social media to give users more control over their feeds. By showing which topics AI thinks a user prefers, Instagram is making its recommendation system more transparent. Users now have the power to remove topics they are no longer interested in or add new ones to better shape their viewing experience.


This level of customization also appears to be part of a larger strategy by Meta, Instagram’s parent company. A similar feature is in development for Meta’s Threads app, which would let users indicate they want more or less of certain types of content. Instagram is also testing new ways for users to provide feedback on Reels or Facebook posts that do not feel relevant. The company said it will continue to develop options that allow users to influence their feeds and refine content suggestions over time.

How to adjust your reels feed

Users who want to make the most of the new feature can start by tapping the icon at the top of a Reels video. Instagram will present a list of AI-identified topics that the algorithm believes are relevant. Users can then remove interests they find irrelevant or add new ones to personalize their feed. They can also share these choices on Instagram Stories, giving followers a glimpse into the topics that shape their experience.

This approach signals a shift toward more transparent algorithms, where users are given tools to guide AI rather than being passive consumers. By offering direct input into the Reels feed, Instagram is attempting to create a more satisfying and tailored experience.

A step toward smarter feeds

Instagram’s new feature aligns with growing concerns over how social media platforms recommend content. Many users have expressed frustration over seeing posts that feel repetitive or irrelevant. Giving people control over their Reels feed addresses these complaints without compromising the AI’s ability to make recommendations.

As users engage with Your Algorithm, they will likely notice a Reels feed that better reflects their evolving interests. The ability to adjust preferences and provide feedback on relevance makes the platform feel more interactive and responsive. For Instagram, this is also an opportunity to keep users engaged by offering a sense of ownership over what appears on their screens.

The rollout of Your Algorithm marks a small but significant change in social media personalization. By letting users actively manage what they see, Instagram is redefining the relationship between AI recommendations and user choice, making the experience feel more intentional and user-driven.

data.title: How smartphone trains AI with everything you do secretly
data.description:
Your smartphone collects data every moment it’s powered on, feeding information to artificial intelligence systems you never agreed to train. This isn’t conspiracy theory but documented practice by major technology companies. The device in your pocket records conversations, photographs everything you see, tracks every location you visit, and analyzes all your digital interactions to improve AI models.

Most people understand that apps collect some data, but the scope and depth of collection extends far beyond typical assumptions. Companies gather keystroke patterns, voice recordings from accidental activations, facial recognition data from photo libraries, and detailed behavioral patterns from how you use apps. This information trains AI systems to predict behavior, recognize patterns, and ultimately influence decisions.


What your smartphone collects while you sleep

Even during sleep hours, smartphones remain active data collection devices. Motion sensors track movement patterns and sleep quality. Microphones listen for wake words but often capture ambient conversations. Location services ping cell towers and wifi networks to maintain precise position data. Background apps refresh and upload collected information to company servers.

The overnight charging period provides ideal data transmission time. Your phone uploads gigabytes of information while connected to power and wifi. This includes photos tagged with facial recognition data, voice assistant queries with full audio recordings, and detailed logs of every app interaction from the previous day. Companies process this information to refine AI models continuously.


How AI learns from your photos and messages

Every photo stored on your smartphone feeds facial recognition algorithms. The AI analyzes faces, objects, locations, and contexts to improve image recognition capabilities. Your vacation photos train systems to identify landmarks. Pictures of meals teach AI to recognize food types. Family photos refine facial recognition accuracy across ages and expressions.

Text messages provide even more valuable training data. AI systems analyze conversation patterns, vocabulary usage, emotional tone, and relationship dynamics. The algorithms learn natural language patterns, slang evolution, and cultural communication differences. Your private messages become training examples for chatbots and language models used by millions.

Settings buried deep that control data collection

Most privacy controls hide several menus deep in settings interfaces. Companies design these paths to discourage users from finding and disabling data collection. Location services contain dozens of individual app permissions that require manual review. Photo access includes options for full library versus selected photos that many users never notice.

Voice assistant settings control whether conversations get saved and reviewed by human employees. This option typically defaults to enabled, meaning real people listen to your recordings to improve AI accuracy. Background app refresh allows constant data transmission that most users don’t realize occurs. Advertising identifiers track behavior across apps unless specifically disabled.

Analytics and diagnostics settings determine whether detailed usage patterns get shared with manufacturers. Keyboard settings control whether typing patterns and learned vocabulary sync to cloud servers. Each setting requires individual attention because blanket privacy controls don’t exist.

The real cost of free apps

Apps offered without purchase price extract value through data collection instead. Free messaging apps analyze conversation content to target advertising. Photo editing apps claim rights to use uploaded images for AI training. Game apps track play patterns to optimize addictive mechanics. Social media platforms monetize every interaction through detailed behavioral profiling.

The privacy policies that users accept without reading grant extensive data collection permissions. These documents, written in legal language spanning dozens of pages, authorize companies to use personal information for AI training. Users effectively agree to become unpaid data laborers whose information generates billions in corporate revenue.

Practical steps to stop the collection

Start by reviewing location permissions for every app. Most apps request location access they don’t need for core functionality. Disable background app refresh for non-essential apps to prevent constant data transmission. Turn off voice assistant features or delete conversation history regularly. Use encrypted messaging apps that don’t analyze content for AI training.

Enable ask-to-track prompts introduced in recent operating system updates. This forces apps to explicitly request tracking permission rather than assuming consent. Review photo access permissions and grant only selected photo access when possible. Disable advertising identifiers in privacy settings to limit cross-app tracking.

Consider alternative app stores and open-source applications that prioritize privacy over data collection. Use virtual private networks to mask internet activity from service providers. Enable auto-delete features for location history and voice recordings. The goal isn’t paranoia but informed control over personal information that feeds AI systems without compensation or explicit consent.

data.title: Easy guide to finding Mega Chimecho in Legends Z-A
data.description:
The Mega Dimension DLC for Pokemon Legends Z-A introduces an exciting new addition to the roster with Mega Chimecho, a Steel and Psychic-type Pokemon that’s quickly becoming a favorite among trainers. This powerful evolution brings enhanced stats and impressive battle capabilities, making it a worthy addition to any competitive team.

Finding Chingling in Hyperspace Wild Zones

Before trainers can obtain Mega Chimecho, they’ll need to start with its pre-evolution form. Chingling appears exclusively in the Mega Dimension DLC, which means purchasing the expansion is necessary to access this Pokemon. The tiny bell-shaped creature can only be found within Hyperspace Wild Zones, special dimensional rifts that appear randomly throughout the game world.


These rifts open for limited periods, creating a sense of urgency when they appear on the map. To access them, trainers must first complete the Hyperdimension Survey Missions, which serve as the gateway to this new content. Once unlocked, these zones provide opportunities to catch rare Pokemon that don’t appear anywhere else in the game.

Entering a Hyperspace Wild Zone requires special items called Donuts, which are prepared by Ansha and Hoopa. When a rift containing Chingling appears, trainers should act quickly to enter and attempt a catch before the window closes.


Evolving Chingling into Chimecho

  1. After successfully catching Chingling, trainers need to focus on building a strong bond with their new Pokemon. The evolution process requires high friendship levels, which can be accelerated by visiting cafes throughout Lumiose City. Taking Chingling to these establishments helps strengthen the relationship between trainer and Pokemon.
  2. Once the friendship level is sufficiently high, the final requirement is simple: level up Chingling during nighttime hours. The game’s day and night cycle plays a crucial role here, so trainers should plan accordingly. With these conditions met, Chingling will evolve into Chimecho, bringing trainers one step closer to the Mega Evolution form.

Locating the Chimechite Mega Stone

The Chimechite Mega Stone represents the key to unlocking Mega Chimecho’s full potential. Finding this rare item requires navigating a specific type of Hyperspace rift that’s more challenging than standard encounters.

Trainers should scan their maps for 2-Star Rifts marked with the designation Unidentified Mega Stone Detected. These special rifts indicate the presence of a Mega Stone guardian inside. Before entering, preparation is essential. Selecting a Donut with at least a +30 Hyperspace Level Bonus ensures adequate time to complete the challenge.

  1. Inside the rift, trainers face a Rogue Mega Chimecho in battle. This encounter differs from typical wild Pokemon battles, as the timer creates additional pressure. The calorie content of the chosen Donut directly affects how long the rift remains open, so higher-quality Donuts provide more breathing room during the encounter.
  2. Unlike other rift types that disappear after a failed attempt, Mega Evolution Rifts remain accessible. This forgiving mechanic allows trainers to retry as many times as needed without losing the opportunity permanently.
  3. Upon defeating the Rogue Mega Chimecho, it drops the Chimechite Mega Stone. Equipping this item to any Chimecho enables Mega Evolution during battles, activated by accumulating Mega Energy and pressing the right stick when ready.

Battle performance and optimal moveset

Mega Chimecho’s transformation brings substantial stat increases across multiple categories. Defense, Special Defense, Special Attack and Speed all receive considerable boosts, transforming this Pokemon into a formidable opponent. While it doesn’t quite reach the heights of powerhouses like Mega Metagross, Mega Chimecho holds its own in both player-versus-environment and player-versus-player battles, earning a Tier A classification in both formats.

The most effective moveset for Mega Chimecho combines Psyshock, Shadow Ball, Dazzling Gleam and Protect. This combination provides excellent type coverage while offering defensive options. Psyshock serves as the primary Psychic-type attack, while Shadow Ball handles Ghost and Psychic opponents. Dazzling Gleam covers Dark and Fighting types, and Protect offers crucial defensive utility.

Trainers seeking additional options can teach Mega Chimecho Steel Beam by locating the Secret TM vendor hidden within Lumiose City’s alleyways. This powerful Steel-type move adds another dimension to the Pokemon’s offensive capabilities, though it comes with recoil damage that requires careful management.

The moderate Speed stat means Protect can’t be spammed as frequently as with faster Pokemon, but the move still provides valuable protection during critical moments. Combined with Mega Chimecho’s improved defensive stats, this creates a Pokemon capable of handling diverse threats across various battle scenarios.

SOURCE: GAMERANT

data.title: AI tools automated my $120k job in 5 simple steps
data.description:
The realization hit during a particularly monotonous week. Eighty percent of work tasks followed predictable patterns that could theoretically be automated. The remaining twenty percent required actual human judgment and creativity. This distribution suggested an obvious solution: let artificial intelligence handle the repetitive majority while focusing human attention on the valuable minority.

The transition didn’t happen overnight. Building effective automation requires understanding which tools handle specific tasks best and how to connect them into seamless workflows. The initial setup demanded significant time investment, but the payoff became apparent within weeks. Tasks that previously consumed hours now complete in minutes, often with better consistency than manual execution.


The communication and data analysis handlers

Email and message management consumed roughly fifteen hours weekly before automation. An AI assistant now reads incoming communications, categorizes them by urgency and topic, drafts appropriate responses, and flags the few messages requiring personal attention. The system learns from corrections over time, improving its accuracy continuously. The key to effective email automation involves creating detailed response templates and decision trees.

Generating weekly reports previously required gathering data from multiple sources, performing calculations, creating visualizations, and writing summaries. Now automation pulls data automatically, runs predetermined analyses, generates charts, and produces written summaries in the company’s standard format. The entire process completes overnight, delivering finished reports by morning. The quality of automated reports often exceeds manually created ones because computers don’t make arithmetic errors or accidentally skip data points.


Schedule management and document creation

Calendar management alone used to consume several hours weekly. Coordinating meeting times across multiple participants, blocking focus time for deep work, and adjusting schedules when priorities shifted created constant administrative burden. Automation now handles this entirely, using AI to understand meeting priorities, participant availability, and project deadlines.

The project management automation tracks task dependencies, deadline approaching, and resource allocation. When projects fall behind schedule, the system automatically adjusts timelines and notifies relevant stakeholders.

Creating presentations, proposals, and documentation consumed significant time. An AI tool now generates first drafts based on brief prompts describing the desired content. While these drafts require human editing and refinement, starting from a complete draft rather than a blank page reduces creation time by approximately seventy percent. The automation excels at maintaining consistent formatting and incorporating company branding.

The integration platform that connects everything

None of these tools would work effectively in isolation. The fifth critical component is an integration platform that connects all the automation tools, allowing them to share data and trigger each other. When a new project gets created, the system automatically generates necessary documents, schedules kickoff meetings, sets up tracking systems, and notifies team members.

This integration creates workflows that rival or exceed human efficiency. The system never forgets steps, always follows procedures correctly, and operates twenty-four hours daily. Projects progress steadily even when humans are sleeping or focusing on other priorities.

Ethics and implementation strategy

Automating a job while collecting a full salary raises obvious questions. The justification lies in what gets done with the freed time. Rather than coasting, that time now goes toward strategic thinking, innovation, and projects that actually create value for the organization. The quality and quantity of meaningful contribution actually increased through automation.

Many companies are slowly realizing that they pay for results, not for hours of keyboard tapping. If automation delivers those results more efficiently, that’s ultimately beneficial for everyone. The alternative is watching competitors gain efficiency advantages while stubbornly insisting humans must do everything manually.

Start by documenting every task you perform for two weeks. Identify the most time-consuming, repetitive activities. Research which AI tools handle those specific tasks effectively. Begin with one small automation, refine it until it works reliably, then gradually expand. The biggest mistake people make is trying to automate everything simultaneously. This creates chaos when multiple systems malfunction at once.

This isn’t about replacing humans. It’s about elevating human contribution from routine execution to strategic thinking. Companies will increasingly value employees who effectively leverage automation to multiply their impact. Understanding how to build and manage these systems becomes more valuable than the ability to execute tasks manually.

data.title: GTA Online update brings mansions and mission creator
data.description:
Grand Theft Auto Online is getting its final major content update of 2025, giving players the chance to showcase their wealth through luxury real estate. Titled A Safehouse in the Hills, the new update lets players purchase sprawling mansions complete with premium amenities and customization options.

The content launched today at 10am GMT UK time, 5am EDT and 2am PDT for US players, and 11am CET for European audiences. The update arrives on PlayStation 4, PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X and S, Xbox One and PC simultaneously.


Players can purchase mansions through Prix Luxury Real Estate, featuring what Rockstar Games describes as sprawling outdoor spaces and meticulously designed interiors. The properties include a state of the art AI assistant to help with professional and personal tasks, positioning these homes as functional bases rather than purely cosmetic additions.

Mansion features and amenities

The new properties come loaded with features designed to appeal to veteran players looking to display their accomplishments. Trophy cabinets let owners showcase achievements while private salons provide entertainment spaces. Animal kennels, elegant garages with indoor car podiums, armories and vehicle workshops offer practical functionality.


Additional amenities include helipads for quick travel, cigar rooms, vaults for storing valuables, media rooms, arcade spaces and yoga areas. The variety aims to make each mansion feel like a complete lifestyle hub rather than just another property on the map.

Optional upgrades let players host their own parties and hire security teams, adding social and protective elements to ownership. These customization choices mean two players could own the same mansion layout but create completely different experiences through their selections.

Beyond real estate

The Safehouse in the Hills update extends beyond property purchases. Rockstar added new law enforcement vehicles and drift variants to the game’s expanding vehicle roster. Freemode events provide additional activities for players exploring the open world, while several experience upgrades improve quality of life across various game modes.

The most significant addition might be the new mission creator, described as an in depth and comprehensive toolkit. Players can design and publish custom missions using the system, creating objectives, placing actors and building scenarios limited mainly by imagination. This user generated content tool could extend the update’s longevity well beyond its initial launch as the community develops and shares creations.

Timing and context

Positioning this update as the final major content drop of 2025 signals Rockstar’s planning for the year ahead. The December timing lets players enjoy new content during the holiday season when gaming activity typically peaks. The wealth showcase theme fits naturally with year end celebration periods when people often indulge in luxury purchases.

The mansion system represents another layer in GTA Online’s evolving property empire. Players have accumulated apartments, businesses, bunkers, facilities, nightclubs, arcades and auto shops over the years. Adding luxury mansions creates a new aspirational tier for those who have completed other property investments and want something to work toward.

The AI assistant feature suggests Rockstar continues exploring ways to streamline player interactions with game systems. Rather than traveling to different locations for various services, the assistant concept centralizes access through mansion ownership. This quality of life improvement could influence how future content handles player convenience versus traditional mission structures.

Community creation tools

The mission creator tool positions GTA Online players as content developers themselves. Similar systems in other games have produced memorable community creations that sometimes rival or exceed official content. Giving players robust tools for building and sharing missions could generate thousands of new experiences without requiring constant developer intervention.

The toolkit’s comprehensive nature suggests Rockstar wants serious creators experimenting with possibilities rather than limiting options to basic scenarios. The more freedom players receive, the more diverse and creative the resulting missions should become. This could establish an ongoing content pipeline as talented community members develop followings for their creations.

The update’s combination of luxury showcasing, new vehicles, improved systems and creation tools aims to provide something for various player types. Whether someone wants to grind for a dream mansion, race new drift cars, host parties or build elaborate missions, the Safehouse in the Hills update offers multiple engagement paths.

data.title: Google, Apple may team up to ease Android-iPhone switching
data.description:
Switching between Android and iPhone has long been a frustrating process. Users often face missing photos, incomplete message histories, and apps that fail to transfer correctly. Now, signs suggest that Google and Apple may be working together to simplify this process, aiming for a smoother, more secure experience.

Early indications of this collaboration appeared in the latest Android Canary build, version 2512, which is available for Google Pixel devices. A new “Copy data” option has appeared under Settings → Profile → All services → Pair with iPhone or iPad. This expands on the existing eSIM-only transfer option in stable Android builds and points to a broader, more comprehensive solution.


A secure transfer workflow

Activating the feature initiates a wireless transfer process using a session ID and passcode. This acts as a secure handshake between the devices. The iPhone needs to run iOS 26, consistent with reports that Apple is preparing a compatible system for an upcoming developer beta.

The option also appears during the Android setup process if users select “Copy data from iPhone” and cannot scan a QR code. This suggests that the feature is designed to be part of the out-of-box experience, making it easier for new users to migrate without additional steps or apps.


While still in development, the feature indicates a shared protocol capable of supporting more types of data. Users may eventually be able to transfer photos, videos, messages, and other personal information without relying on third-party solutions.

Impact on device switching

If fully implemented, this collaboration could represent the largest improvement in cross-platform switching in years. Many users have hesitated to switch ecosystems due to concerns about lost data or complex processes. A polished and reliable solution may remove one of the biggest barriers to changing devices.

Google and Apple have confirmed that the initial rollout will be in Android beta versions before a wider release. While a full timeline has not been provided, the development signals a shift toward better interoperability and user-friendly device transitions.

Looking ahead

This joint effort may make device loyalty less about ecosystem constraints and more about user preference. By creating a more seamless experience, Google and Apple could redefine expectations for cross-platform migration, making it easier for users to switch phones when they want.

The move also reflects a growing focus on user experience in the competitive smartphone market. Users who previously avoided switching may find themselves more open to trying new devices without fear of losing important data.

The appearance of the new Copy data option in Android Canary builds offers an early glimpse of what could be a major improvement in device migration. While the feature is unfinished, it points to a future where moving between Android and iPhone is faster, more reliable, and more secure. As both companies continue development, users may soon find it easier to change phones without worrying about missing photos, messages, or other important content.

The collaboration between Google and Apple shows that even competing tech giants recognize the importance of user-friendly solutions, and it may finally make switching ecosystems a simple, stress-free process.
